---
title: "Projects"
---

A collection of data science projects focused on causal inference, experimentation, and applied machine learning.

---

## NBA 65-Game Rule — Causal Analysis of Injury Rates

**Methods:** Difference-in-Differences
**Domain:** Sports Analytics
**Status:** In progress

The NBA introduced a 65-game minimum in the 2023–24 season to qualify for end-of-season awards. This project investigates whether the rule change had the unintended consequence of increasing injury rates among star players, using a DiD design that compares star players to a control group of "sub-star" players before and after the rule's implementation.

---

## Netflix Market Entry & Genre Box Office

**Methods:** Difference-in-Differences
**Domain:** Entertainment / Media Economics
**Status:** Data collection in progress

When Netflix entered a new country, did it differentially suppress certain genres at the theatrical box office? This project uses Netflix's staggered international expansion as a natural experiment, combining IMDb genre data with European box office admissions from the Lumiere database.

---

*More projects coming soon.*
